title: uragoga subcrocea
intro: Uragoga subcrocea is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids. Uragoga subcrocea is known for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and utilization in traditional medicine.
